title: H-A2 timestamp-clean original-entry revision preregistration
purpose: Pre-register a local no-paid H-A2 follow-up that removes the 15-minute conflict component and tests only features known by the original 09:35 ET entry decision.
source_decision_summary: H-A2.30 found the locked 09:45 delayed-entry condition is timestamp-clean but delayed-entry implementable PnL is not computable because no auditable 09:45 option quote/fill exists in current artifacts. The next safe original-entry path is to pre-register a 09:35-known feature set before any new run.
research_question: Can H-A2 be restated as a timestamp-clean original-entry rule using only information known by 09:35 ET, without reusing the 15-minute conflict component or adding an OOS-selected filter?

allowed_features.rule: May use only labels that are known before or at the entry decision timestamp.
May test threshold 0.001 as already locked; no new threshold search.

forbidden_features.reason: Not knowable at original 09:35 ET entry.
Belongs to a separate delayed-entry quote acquisition branch.

planned_future_tests.question: Does every feature used by the original-entry revision become known at or before 09:35 ET?
How many train/OOS trades remain after the 09:35-known rule without changing threshold 0.001?
Does the original-entry revision retain useful implementable PnL using actual 09:35 original-entry trade outcomes?
Does the revised original-entry rule survive basic big-day, concentration, MinTRL/PSR, and DSR/search-log checks?
Should H-A2 continue as timestamp-clean original-entry research, return to delayed-entry quote acquisition, or be parked?

continue_original_entry_revision_if: All used features are timestamp-clean by 09:35 ET.
OOS retained results remain directionally useful without changing threshold 0.001.
The result is honestly labeled under-sampled/underpowered when applicable.
return_to_delayed_quote_acquisition_if: The original-entry revision loses the observed diagnostic signal.
The economic logic requires the 15-minute confirmation to avoid false positives.
Delayed-entry quote acquisition can be planned inside guardrails.
park_h_a2_if: The retained sample collapses below a useful diagnostic level.
Implementable PnL reverses or cost drag consumes the apparent edge.
The mechanism can no longer be stated without OOS-selected filters.

allowed_claims: H-A2 has a pre-registered timestamp-clean original-entry revision ready for a local E1 diagnostic test.
The revision may use only features known by 09:35 ET.
The future run may produce E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only.
Threshold 0.001 remains locked unless a separate future preregistration explicitly defines a new train-only rule.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim H-A2 edge is validated.
Do not claim E2 acceptance-grade evidence.
Do not use the 15-minute conflict component for an original 09:35 entry decision.
Do not treat original 09:35 PnL as delayed-entry PnL.
Do not change threshold 0.001 through OOS search.
Do not add a new OOS-selected filter.
Do not request IBKR bars from this preregistration.
Do not buy new data from this preregistration.
Do not call LLMs from this preregistration.
Do not retry GDELT from this preregistration.
future_run_completion_criteria: The future run proves all used features are known by 09:35 ET or blocks the original-entry claim.
The future run proves the 15-minute conflict component is excluded.
The future run reports train/OOS retained and skipped counts.
The future run preserves threshold 0.001 and records no new OOS-selected filter.
The future run reports sample-adequacy labels and keeps evidence capped at E1.
The future run writes research log 031 if it completes as a real experiment.
tier_blockers: Future run can be E1 diagnostic/prioritization evidence only
